Daily DAP Accessibility Report — 2026-04-17#

Run ID: run-2026-04-17-fc96a35361 · Status: success · Source data: 2026-04-15 · Generated: 2026-04-17T09:49:45.646Z

View HTML/CSS/JS Code Quality report for this date →

About These Reports#

The Digital Analytics Program (DAP) is a U.S. government analytics service that collects website traffic data across participating federal agencies. DAP tracks page views, visitor counts, and usage patterns for hundreds of government websites, providing transparency into how the public engages with federal digital services. The traffic data is publicly available at analytics.usa.gov.

This report measures the quality and accessibility of the top 100 most-visited U.S. government URLs as reported by DAP. Each day, Lighthouse scans are run against these URLs to measure:

  • Performance - How fast pages load for users (scores 0-100, higher is better)
  • Accessibility - How well pages work for users with disabilities, following WCAG guidelines (scores 0-100, higher is better)
  • Best Practices - Whether pages follow modern web development standards (scores 0-100, higher is better)
  • SEO - How well pages are optimized for search engines (scores 0-100, higher is better)

Accessibility findings come from axe-core, the industry-standard accessibility testing engine embedded in Lighthouse. The axe findings surface specific WCAG violations such as missing alternative text, insufficient color contrast, and missing form labels that make government websites harder to use for people with disabilities.

Traffic data reflects daily visitor counts from DAP. URLs are ranked by page load count, ensuring the most-used government pages are prioritized for quality measurement.

Accessibility Trend Narrative#

Over the past 30 days of data (2026-03-18 to 2026-04-16), government website accessibility scores have remained stable (-0.17 points, from 92.66 to 92.49). Performance scores have declined (-3.01 points, from 55.32 to 52.31).

Today's aggregate accessibility score of 92.36 reflects the mean Lighthouse accessibility score across 99 successfully scanned government URLs. A score above 90 indicates generally strong compliance with WCAG automated checks, though manual testing is always recommended to fully assess accessibility.

The following axe-core rules were most frequently violated across scanned URLs today. These patterns indicate systemic accessibility barriers present across multiple government websites.

Human impact narratives for top issues

The following narratives describe the real-world impact of each accessibility barrier on people with disabilities. These barriers are not abstract technical failures; they prevent citizens from independently accessing government services.

target-size: Digital Motor Access

Small touch targets act as a digital gatekeeper, excluding individuals with tremors, arthritis, or limited dexterity from accessing essential services independently. These technical failures transform a routine interaction into a source of failure, stripping away the autonomy of citizens who require a frictionless, accessible interface to participate in digital life. The approximately 58 million Americans with ambulatory or self-care disabilities are disproportionately impacted by inadequate touch target sizing on government mobile websites.

Affected groups:

  • People with Parkinson's disease, arthritis, or hand tremors
  • Older adults with reduced fine motor control
  • People with motor disabilities using alternative pointing devices
  • People in situational impairment contexts (e.g., commuting, holding a child)

landmark-one-main: Primary Content Identification

Without a main landmark, screen reader users lose their most efficient way to skip to the primary content of a government page. Every page visit requires tabbing through the entire navigation structure to reach the actual content, a significant time and effort burden that accumulates across repeated visits to government websites for citizens managing ongoing benefit cases or legal matters.

Affected groups:

  • People who are blind using screen readers
  • People who are deaf and rely on visual-to-text tools
  • People with motor disabilities using keyboard navigation

heading-order: Document Structure Navigation

Screen reader users navigate complex government websites primarily through heading structure, using headings as a table of contents to jump between sections. Skipped heading levels break the logical document outline, causing confusion about the hierarchy of information and forcing users to re-read sections to understand the relationship between topics, adding significant time and effort to information-gathering tasks.

Affected groups:

  • People who are blind using screen readers
  • People who are deaf and rely on visual-to-text tools
  • People with motor disabilities using keyboard navigation

color-contrast: Visual Information Access

Low contrast text is one of the most pervasive barriers on government websites, rendering critical information invisible to the approximately 26 million Americans with low vision or color blindness. When agency announcements, form instructions, error messages, or legal notices are displayed in insufficient contrast, affected citizens are denied equal access to the information they need to exercise their rights and access public services.

Affected groups:

  • People with low vision including age-related vision loss
  • People who are color blind (approximately 8% of men, 0.5% of women)

label-content-name-mismatch: Voice Control Activation

When the accessible name of an element does not match its visible text, voice control users cannot activate it using the words they see on screen. Saying "click Submit" to a button whose visual label is "Submit" but whose accessible name is "submitbtn" results in no action. This mismatch silently breaks voice control for citizens with motor disabilities who depend on this input method to interact with government websites.

Affected groups:

  • People who are blind using screen readers
  • People with low vision relying on screen magnifiers
  • People who are deaf using keyboard navigation
  • People with motor disabilities using voice control software

link-name: Link Purpose Clarity

Links without accessible names are completely useless to screen reader users who navigate government pages by jumping between links. An unnamed link could lead anywhere, and activating it unknowingly could trigger downloads, open unexpected pages, or initiate unintended processes. On government websites, unnamed links undermine the informed consent principle by preventing citizens from knowing where a link will take them.

Affected groups:

  • People who are blind using screen readers
  • People who are deaf and rely on visual-to-text tools
  • People with motor disabilities using keyboard navigation

tabindex: Focus Order Predictability

Positive tabindex values create unpredictable focus sequences that jump around the page in ways that differ from the visual reading order. For keyboard users and screen reader users on government websites, an erratic tab order makes it difficult to reliably navigate forms and interactive content, increasing errors and the time needed to complete tasks, and creating particular frustration for users with cognitive disabilities who need a predictable, logical interaction flow.

Affected groups:

  • People who are blind using screen readers
  • People who are deaf using keyboard-only navigation
  • People with motor disabilities using keyboard or switch access

image-alt: Visual Content Access

Images without alternative text are completely invisible to blind users and to search engines. On government websites, informational images, diagrams, infographics, and document screenshots without alt text deprive blind citizens of the same information available to sighted visitors. Critical details like emergency instructions, benefit eligibility charts, or form procedure diagrams can be entirely inaccessible, putting these citizens at a disadvantage in accessing government services and information.

Affected groups:

  • People who are blind using screen readers
  • People who are deaf and rely on visual-to-text tools

html-has-lang: Language Identification

Without a language attribute, screen readers default to the user's system language settings, which may cause government content to be read in the wrong language with incorrect pronunciation. This is particularly harmful for multilingual citizens accessing government services in their non-primary language, and for users of braille displays that depend on language metadata for proper rendering.

Affected groups:

  • People who are blind using screen readers
  • People who are deaf and rely on visual-to-text tools
  • People with cognitive or language differences

aria-allowed-attr: Assistive Technology Reliability

Invalid ARIA attributes cause assistive technologies to misinterpret or malfunction on interactive controls. For screen reader users navigating complex government forms or applications, broken ARIA creates unpredictable behavior that can make tasks impossible to complete, denying access to essential services.

Affected groups:

  • People who are blind using screen readers
  • People who are deaf and rely on visual-to-text assistive tools
  • People with motor disabilities using switch access or voice control
Disability icon key
Without Vision — People who are blind or have no functional vision (1.0% of U.S. population — ~3,400,000 Americans)
Limited Vision — People with low vision who need magnification or high contrast (2.4% of U.S. population — ~8,100,000 Americans)
Without Perception of Color — People who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness) (4.3% of U.S. population — ~14,500,000 Americans)
Without Hearing — People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content (0.3% of U.S. population — ~1,100,000 Americans)
Limited Hearing — People with hearing loss who may struggle with audio without accommodations (3.5% of U.S. population — ~11,900,000 Americans)
Without Speech — People who cannot use speech or voice-based input effectively (0.5% of U.S. population — ~1,700,000 Americans)
Limited Manipulation — People with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity (2.2% of U.S. population — ~7,600,000 Americans)
Limited Reach and Strength — People with limited reach, strength, or stamina (5.8% of U.S. population — ~19,600,000 Americans)
Limited Language, Cognitive, and Learning Abilities — People with cognitive, learning, or language differences (4.7% of U.S. population — ~15,900,000 Americans)

These icons show which groups of people with disabilities are excluded by each accessibility barrier. Where page view data is available, each icon shows an estimated number of people potentially excluded (page loads for affected URLs × disability prevalence rate from U.S. Census ACS 2023). Hover over or focus an icon to see the full estimate and methodology. Icons follow the Section 508 Functional Performance Criteria and the equivalent EU EN 301 549 v3.2.1 Table B.2 categories.

Prevalence data: U.S. Census Bureau, American Community Survey (ACS) 2023 1-Year Estimates, Table B18101; supplemental data from CDC, NIDCD, AFB, and NIH/NEI. U.S. population base: ~336,500,000 (2023 estimate). Reviewed annually.

See the Section 508 WCAG to FPC mapping for additional detail on how accessibility requirements map to functional needs.

Usability Heuristics Summary#

The most common accessibility violations map to Nielsen Norman Group's 10 usability heuristics. This table shows which heuristics are most affected by today's top axe-core violations, helping to identify where systemic usability barriers exist.

Americans Excluded by Disability Today#

Based on 22,403,856 page loads across successfully scanned government URLs and U.S. disability prevalence rates from the U.S. Census Bureau, American Community Survey (ACS) 2023 1-Year Estimates, Table B18101 (2023), an estimated 1,730,000 Americans encountered an accessibility barrier on a government website today.

Each row shows the number of people in a disability group who visited a page with at least one accessibility issue that affects their group. Prevalence rates and U.S. population estimates are derived from the American Community Survey (ACS) and supplemental sources. These figures are rough estimates intended to illustrate the scale of accessibility barriers.

Estimated Americans excluded today by disability category (2023 Census data)

Without Perception of Color 4.3% ~14,500,000 8,500,000 360,000

Limited Vision 2.4% ~8,100,000 10,900,000 260,000
Without Vision 1.0% ~3,400,000 14,300,000 140,000
Limited Language, Cognitive, and Learning Abilities 4.7% ~15,900,000 1,750,000 82,331
Without Hearing 0.3% ~1,100,000 14,300,000 42,918

Sources: U.S. Census Bureau, American Community Survey (ACS) 2023 1-Year Estimates, Table B18101; disability prevalence from ACS 2023 and supplemental CDC/NIDCD/NIH data. Reviewed annually.

Performance Impact on Americans#

Google defines a good Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) as under 2.5 seconds and recommends pages under 1.6 MB. The figures below estimate how much extra time Americans spend waiting, and how much extra data is transferred, because government websites fall short of these benchmarks. Calculations are based on 99 successfully scanned URLs with Lighthouse timing data.

Estimated performance impact vs. Google benchmarks for government URLs scanned on April 17, 2026
Metric Estimated total (April 17, 2026) Notes
Extra time waiting (vs 2.5s LCP benchmark) 3 years, 1 month, 6 daysExtra time is calculated as: for each scanned URL, max(0, actual LCP − 2.5s) × page loads. Total: 97,860,392 seconds. 97,860,392 seconds
Extra data transferred (vs 1.6 MB page weight benchmark) ~25.2 TB (1,047 copies of Wikipedia)Extra data is calculated as: for each scanned URL, max(0, actual page weight − 1.6 MB) × page loads. Across 99 URLs with weight data

LCP and page weight are measured by Lighthouse. Wikipedia copy count uses a size of 24.05 GB per Wikipedia:Size of Wikipedia. These are rough estimates based on a sample of the top government URLs by traffic. Hover or focus the estimated totals for calculation details.

Content Density (Words per Megabyte)#

Words-per-Megabyte (WpM) measures how efficiently a page delivers readable content relative to its total download size. A low ratio indicates Digital Bloat—the page transfers far more data than the text it contains. Pages below 200 WpM are flagged for optimization review.

  • URLs with content metrics: 70
  • Average word count: 675
  • Average Words/MB: 591
  • URLs below 200 WpM threshold: 61
Content density for top URLs scanned on April 17, 2026 (up to 10 shown)

https://weather.gov 76 59
https://uscis.gov 172 23
https://spc.noaa.gov 429 33
https://cdc.gov 242 77

Word counts are extracted from the page’s main article content using Mozilla Readability, which strips navigation, sidebars, and ads. Page weight is the total resource size measured by Lighthouse. Pages below 200 WpM may impose unnecessary data costs on users on metered connections.

Detected Technologies#

Technology signals detected from the network requests loaded by each scanned URL. CMS detection identifies WordPress, Drupal, Joomla, ASP.NET, SharePoint, Squarespace, Adobe Experience Manager, and TYPO3 from characteristic asset paths. USWDS detection identifies use of the U.S. Web Design System.

CMS platform counts and average Lighthouse scores (where available) across 99 successfully scanned URLs
CMS URLs Share Avg AccessibilityAvg PerformanceAvg Best PracticesAvg SEO
Drupal 24Sites: acquisition.gov, archives.gov, cbp.gov, cms.gov, energy.gov, epa.gov, faa.gov, fdic.gov, fns.usda.gov, fs.usda.gov, home.treasury.gov, justice.gov, medicare.gov, noaa.gov, ofac.treasury.gov, osha.gov, sam.gov, sba.gov, studentaid.gov, tsa.gov, usa.gov, uscis.gov, usitc.gov, www.hud.gov 24% 96 45 82 91
WordPress 3Sites: nasa.gov, science.nasa.gov, state.gov 3% 95 52 82 87
Adobe Experience Manager 2Sites: census.gov, travel.state.gov 2% 89 38 48 84
ASP.NET 2Sites: armypubs.army.mil, exclusions.oig.hhs.gov 2% 85 63 79 75

USWDS detected on 35 of 99 scanned URLs (35%).

Observed USWDS versions: 2.9.0Sites: fbi.gov, 3.7.1Sites: myhealth.va.gov, 3.8.0Sites: fdic.gov. The latest release is available at github.com/uswds/uswds/releases.

Third-Party JavaScript Services#

Third-party scripts detected from network requests loaded by scanned pages. These services may affect page performance, user privacy, and security posture.

11 services marked ⚠ tracking send user data to third-party servers and may have privacy implications for site visitors.

Accessibility Statements (M-24-08)#

OMB Memorandum M-24-08 requires each federal agency to publish a digital accessibility statement that includes contact information for accessibility issues, known limitations, and a link to the agency Section 508 program page.

65 of 99 domains (66%) checked have a detectable accessibility statement at a standard URL path.

Required Federal Links (M-17-06)#

OMB Memorandum M-17-06 and the 21st Century IDEA Act require federal agency public websites to provide links to a Privacy Policy, Contact page, and FOIA page. These were among the compliance criteria benchmarked by the performance.gov federal website performance initiative (no longer active, but the underlying requirements remain in force).

63 of 99 domains (64%) have all three required link types detectable at standard URL paths.

Section 508: Legal Requirements vs. Best Practices#

Section 508 of the Rehabilitation Act was enacted in 1998 and requires U.S. federal agencies to make their electronic and information technology accessible to people with disabilities. The technical standards were significantly updated through the ICT Accessibility Standards refresh, which became effective in January 2018 — the same year Apple celebrated the 10th anniversary of the iPhone. That refresh incorporated WCAG 2.0 Level AA (published in 2008) as the baseline for web content.

The web has changed dramatically since then. What agencies are legally required to meet today is built on a standard from 2008 — the same era as the original iPhone. Agencies should treat WCAG 2.0 AA as the floor, not the ceiling.

Legal Requirement

WCAG 2.0 Level AA (published 2008)

Referenced by the Section 508 ICT Standards refresh (effective 2018). Agencies must meet these 38 success criteria to satisfy their Section 508 obligations for web content.

  • Text alternatives for non-text content
  • Captions and audio descriptions for multimedia
  • Keyboard accessibility and focus management
  • Minimum color contrast (4.5:1 for normal text)
  • No seizure-inducing content
  • Consistent navigation and labeling
  • Error identification and correction

Section 508 web content requirements (E205)

Best Practices

WCAG 2.1 and 2.2 Level AA (published 2018 and 2023)

These later versions add criteria that reflect how the web actually works today — mobile devices, cognitive accessibility, and modern authentication patterns — and are strongly recommended for any agency aiming to serve all Americans. The US Federal Government is one of the last to use the legacy WCAG 2.0 AA Standard, as it was developed ~2 decades ago.

  • WCAG 2.1 (2018): 17 new criteria for mobile, low vision, and cognitive accessibility
  • WCAG 2.2 (2023): 9 more criteria for authentication and cognitive tasks, removing the outdated "Parsing" criterion
  • W3C now recommends WCAG 2.2 as the current normative standard for new and updated content

What is new in WCAG 2.2 · WCAG 2.2 specification

The scores and findings in this report are based on automated checks aligned with WCAG 2.x rules surfaced by axe-core. Passing automated checks is necessary but not sufficient: manual testing with assistive technologies is required to verify true conformance and to catch barriers that automation cannot detect.

Take Action#

Today's scan identified 208 accessibility barriers across the most-visited U.S. government websites, affecting an estimated 1,730,000 Americans with disabilities. Here is how you can help.

  • Read the federal accessibility report. The 2025 Governmentwide Section 508 Assessment from the GSA Administrator details the state of federal accessibility compliance and the steps agencies are taking to improve.
  • Submit URLs you care about. Want specific government pages included in future scans? Submit them to the Open Scans project to help broaden coverage.
  • Test with free tools. Do your own automated and manual accessibility testing with Accessibility Insights and Google Lighthouse. Automated tools catch a significant portion of WCAG failures quickly; manual review with assistive technologies catches the rest.
  • Adopt and update the U.S. Web Design System (USWDS). The USWDS is already a strong foundation for accessible, consistent federal websites, but many agencies have not adopted it or are running outdated versions. Encourage your agency to adopt or upgrade so every American benefits from its accessibility work.
  • Hire people with disabilities. People with disabilities are the ultimate experts on digital barriers. Bringing them into design, engineering, and testing teams — not just as consultants but as full-time employees — leads to more accessible products and services for everyone.

https://ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 91

Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast) DAP-84eeaaec

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Disabilities affected: ~98KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~98,127 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 4,088,607 affected page loads) ~175KPeople who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~175,810 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 4,088,607 affected page loads)

<p class="date">

Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size) DAP-ebb2bc0f

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Element 1 DAP-5d46b830

Element path: div.row > div.col-lg-3 > p > a.text-white

Snippet:

<a href="https://www.nlm.nih.gov/accessibility.html" class="text-white">

Label: Accessibility

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (77.3px by 15px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 23.8px instead of at least 24px.

  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 23.8px instead of at least 24px.

`[aria-*]` attributes do not match their roles (rule: aria-allowed-attr) DAP-4bdf91e9

Each ARIA `role` supports a specific subset of `aria-*` attributes. Mismatching these invalidates the `aria-*` attributes. Learn how to match ARIA attributes to their roles.

Element 1 DAP-1aacc3db

Element path: div#usps-carousel > div.carousel-inner > ol.carousel-indicators > li.active

Snippet:

<li data-target="#usps-carousel" data-slide-to="0" aria-checked="true" class="active">

Label: Slide 1

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • ARIA attribute is not allowed: aria-checked="true"

Element path: div#usps-carousel > div.carousel-inner > ol.carousel-indicators > li

<li data-target="#usps-carousel" data-slide-to="1" aria-checked="false">

  • ARIA attribute is not allowed: aria-checked="false"

Element path: div#usps-carousel > div.carousel-inner > ol.carousel-indicators > li

Snippet:

<li data-target="#usps-carousel" data-slide-to="2" aria-checked="false">

  • ARIA attribute is not allowed: aria-checked="false"

Element 4 DAP-19fe08e3

Element path: div#usps-carousel > div.carousel-inner > ol.carousel-indicators > li

Snippet:

<li data-target="#usps-carousel" data-slide-to="3" aria-checked="false">

Label: Slide 4

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • ARIA attribute is not allowed: aria-checked="false"
Elements with an ARIA `[role]` that require children to contain a specific `[role]` are missing some or all of those required children. (rule: aria-required-children) DAP-29ea0262

Some ARIA parent roles must contain specific child roles to perform their intended accessibility functions. Learn more about roles and required children elements.

Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt) DAP-474d58d2

Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. Learn more about the `alt` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~10KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~10,120 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 1,012,036 affected page loads) ~3KPeople who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Element 1 DAP-8e1cf442

Snippet:

<img src="https://beacon.lynx.cognitivlabs.com/pixel/capture?tags=8dd8cf55-3ae9-49dc…">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have an alt attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name) DAP-f1542b28

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

List items (`<li>`) are not contained within `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` parent elements. (rule: listitem) DAP-2be6feaa

`[user-scalable="no"]` is used in the `<meta name="viewport">` element or the `[maximum-scale]` attribute is less than 5. (rule: meta-viewport) DAP-7b9fc94e

Disabling zooming is problematic for users with low vision who rely on screen magnification to properly see the contents of a web page. Learn more about the viewport meta tag.

Disabilities affected: ~24KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~24,289 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 1,012,036 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-efeb19cc

Element path: head > meta

Snippet:

<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, maximum-scale=1.0, user-scalable=no">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • user-scalable=no on <meta> tag disables zooming on mobile devices
Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size) DAP-4f9c3fe1

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

https://forecast.weather.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 67

Axe findings: 5

Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast) DAP-7b4fd568

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Disabilities affected: ~21KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~21,997 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 916,546 affected page loads) ~39KPeople who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~39,411 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 916,546 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-7263ff7e

Element path: div.one-sixth-first > div#forecast-lookup > form#getForecast > input#inputstring

Snippet:

<input id="inputstring" name="inputstring" type="text" value="Enter location ..." onclick="this.value=''" autocomplete="off">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has insufficient color contrast of 2.84 (foreground color: #999999, background color: #ffffff, font size: 8.0pt (10.6667px), font weight: normal). Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1
`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute (rule: html-has-lang) DAP-e62a32c4

If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. Learn more about the `lang` attribute.

Estimated ~43,078 people potentially excluded (4.7% prevalence × 916,546 affected page loads)

Document does not have a main landmark. (rule: landmark-one-main) DAP-0d1f4270

One main landmark helps screen reader users navigate a web page. Learn more about landmarks.

Accessibility Findings

https://tools.usps.com

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 68

Axe findings: 10

`button`, `link`, and `menuitem` elements do not have accessible names. (rule: aria-command-name) DAP-c26fc51b

When an element doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it with a generic name, making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. Learn how to make command elements more accessible.

Affected elements (3):

Element 1 DAP-7ca376ec

Element path: div.panel-heading > h4.panel-title > a#headingOneAnchor > span.down-arr

Snippet:

<span role="button" data-parent="#accordion" aria-expanded="true" aria-controls="collapseOne" class="down-arr">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute

Element 2 DAP-958bc2d3

Element path: div.panel-heading > h4.panel-title > a.collapsed > span.down-arr

Snippet:

<span role="button" data-parent="#accordion" aria-expanded="true" aria-controls="collapseOne" class="down-arr">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute

Element 3 DAP-958bc2d3

Element path: div.panel-heading > h4.panel-title > a.collapsed > span.down-arr

Snippet:

<span role="button" data-parent="#accordion" aria-expanded="true" aria-controls="collapseOne" class="down-arr">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
Elements with an ARIA `[role]` that require children to contain a specific `[role]` are missing some or all of those required children. (rule: aria-required-children) DAP-82af62ae

Some ARIA parent roles must contain specific child roles to perform their intended accessibility functions. Learn more about roles and required children elements.

`[role]`s are not contained by their required parent element (rule: aria-required-parent) DAP-b319271f

Some ARIA child roles must be contained by specific parent roles to properly perform their intended accessibility functions. Learn more about ARIA roles and required parent element.

Affected elements (8):

Element 1 DAP-c6b954dd

Element path: div.panel > div.panel-heading > h4.panel-title > a#headingOneAnchor

Snippet:

<a role="button" href="#" tabindex="0" data-parent="#accordion" aria-expanded="true" aria-controls="collapseOne" class="collapsed" id="headingOneAnchor">

Label: What does my tracking number look like?

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Invalid ARIA attribute value: aria-controls="collapseOne"

Element 2 DAP-bcdd7731

Element path: div.panel-heading > h4.panel-title > a#headingOneAnchor > span.down-arr

Snippet:

<span role="button" data-parent="#accordion" aria-expanded="true" aria-controls="collapseOne" class="down-arr">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Invalid ARIA attribute value: aria-controls="collapseOne"

Element 3 DAP-ff899948

Element path: div.col-sm-12 > div#accordion > div.panel > div.panel-heading

Snippet:

<div class="panel-heading collapser" role="tab" data-parent="#accordion" aria-expanded="true" aria-controls="collapseone">

Label: Where do I find my tracking number?

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Invalid ARIA attribute value: aria-controls="collapseone"

Element path: div.panel > div.panel-heading > h4.panel-title > a.collapsed

Snippet:

<a role="button" href="#" tabindex="0" data-parent="#accordion" aria-expanded="true" aria-controls="collapseone" class="collapsed">

`[aria-*]` attributes are not valid or misspelled (rule: aria-valid-attr) DAP-2c8784ed

Assistive technologies, like screen readers, can't interpret ARIA attributes with invalid names. Learn more about valid ARIA attributes.

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order) DAP-1abfb3a4

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name) DAP-8c4fed15

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

Affected elements (2):

Element 1 DAP-2517b980

Element path: div#tracking_page_wrapper > div#g-navigation > div.nav-full > a#endnav

Snippet:

<a name="endnav" id="endnav" href="#" class="hidden-skip">

Label:  

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Element is in tab order and does not have accessible text
  • Fix any of the following:
  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute

Element 2 DAP-500d47a7

Element path: body.tracking_reskin > div#global-footer--wrap > footer.global-footer > a.global-footer--logo-link

Snippet:

<a href="https://www.usps.com/" class="global-footer--logo-link">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Element is in tab order and does not have accessible text
  • Fix any of the following:
  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
List items (`<li>`) are not contained within `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` parent elements. (rule: listitem) DAP-dd951b79

Screen readers require list items (`<li>`) to be contained within a parent `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` to be announced properly. Learn more about proper list structure.

Links rely on color to be distinguishable. (rule: link-in-text-block) DAP-817c69a4

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Link text that is discernible improves the experience for users with low vision. Learn how to make links distinguishable.

Snippet:

<a href="/loan-simulator">

Label: explore and apply for other repayment plans

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 1.64:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #0000ee, surrounding text: #212529)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text

https://ssa.gov

Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast) DAP-a3011ec2

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Disabilities affected: ~12KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~12,909 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 537,891 affected page loads) ~23KPeople who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~23,129 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 537,891 affected page loads)

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order) DAP-d9c1ce44

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Disabilities affected: ~5KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~5,379 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 537,891 affected page loads) ~1KPeople who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~1,614 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 537,891 affected page loads) ~11KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~11,834 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 537,891 affected page loads)

Accessibility Findings

https://egov.uscis.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 90

Axe findings: 1

The document uses `<meta http-equiv="refresh">` (rule: meta-refresh) DAP-cc3b4efb

Users do not expect a page to refresh automatically, and doing so will move focus back to the top of the page. This may create a frustrating or confusing experience. Learn more about the refresh meta tag.

Disabilities affected: ~3KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~3,376 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 337,588 affected page loads) ~1KPeople who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~1,013 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 337,588 affected page loads) ~7KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~7,427 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 337,588 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-b9e04d3c

Element path: head > meta

Snippet:

<meta http-equiv="refresh" content="360">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • <meta> tag forces timed refresh of page (less than 20 hours)

Accessibility Findings

https://weather.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 67

Axe findings: 5

Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast) DAP-86bc430e

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Disabilities affected: ~8KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~8,094 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 337,240 affected page loads) ~14KPeople who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~14,501 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 337,240 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-91b3be38

Element path: div.one-sixth-first > div#forecast-lookup > form#getForecast > input#inputstring

Snippet:

<input id="inputstring" name="inputstring" type="text" value="Enter location ..." onclick="this.value=''" autocomplete="off">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has insufficient color contrast of 2.84 (foreground color: #999999, background color: #ffffff, font size: 8.0pt (10.6667px), font weight: normal). Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1
`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute (rule: html-has-lang) DAP-0d01a89e

If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. Learn more about the `lang` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~3KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~3,372 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 337,240 affected page loads) ~1KPeople who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~1,012 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 337,240 affected page loads) ~15KPeople with cognitive, learning, or language differences. Estimated ~15,850 people potentially excluded (4.7% prevalence × 337,240 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-b7c14034

Element path: html

Snippet:

<html xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The <html> element does not have a lang attribute
Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt) DAP-f0437918

Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. Learn more about the `alt` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~3KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~3,372 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 337,240 affected page loads) ~1KPeople who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~1,012 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 337,240 affected page loads)

Affected elements (3):

Element 1 DAP-82cd40dd

Element path: div.div-full > div#wwamap > div.USMap > img#us

Snippet:

<img id="us" src="https://www.weather.gov/wwamap/png/US.png" usemap="#US_large_imagemap_800_500">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have an alt attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

Element 2 DAP-f1fbd8ef

Element path: div.div-full > div#wwamap > div.USMap > img#alaska

Snippet:

<img id="alaska" src="https://www.weather.gov/wwamap/png/ak.png" usemap="#alaska_imagemap_130_115">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have an alt attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

Element 3 DAP-f9471b99

Element path: div.div-full > div#wwamap > div.USMap > img#hawaii

Snippet:

<img id="hawaii" src="https://www.weather.gov/wwamap/png/hi.png" usemap="#hawaii_imagemap">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have an alt attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size) DAP-8694972a

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Disabilities affected: ~7KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~7,419 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 337,240 affected page loads) ~19KPeople with limited reach, strength, or stamina. Estimated ~19,560 people potentially excluded (5.8% prevalence × 337,240 affected page loads)

  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 11.4px instead of at least 24px.

Document does not have a main landmark. (rule: landmark-one-main) DAP-d40c6891

One main landmark helps screen reader users navigate a web page. Learn more about landmarks.

Accessibility Findings

https://uscis.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 93

Axe findings: 2

`[aria-hidden="true"]` elements contain focusable descendents (rule: aria-hidden-focus) DAP-056620d5

Focusable descendents within an `[aria-hidden="true"]` element prevent those interactive elements from being available to users of assistive technologies like screen readers. Learn how `aria-hidden` affects focusable elements.

Disabilities affected: ~3KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~3,280 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 328,020 affected page loads) ~7KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~7,872 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 328,020 affected page loads) ~984People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~984 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 328,020 affected page loads) ~7KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~7,216 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 328,020 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-16a409b4

Element path: div.view-content > div.slick-list > div.slick-track > div#slick-slide00

Snippet:

<div class="slick-slide" data-slick-index="0" aria-hidden="true" role="tabpanel" id="slick-slide00" tabindex="-1" style="width: 412px;" aria-describedby="slick-slide-control00">

Label: Making America Safe Again USCIS’ end-of-year review demonstrates the impact of…

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Focusable content should have tabindex="-1" or be removed from the DOM
Elements with an ARIA `[role]` that require children to contain a specific `[role]` are missing some or all of those required children. (rule: aria-required-children) DAP-4ac63560

Some ARIA parent roles must contain specific child roles to perform their intended accessibility functions. Learn more about roles and required children elements.

Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio. (rule: color-contrast) DAP-9e537039

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast.

Disabilities affected: ~5KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~5,530 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 230,415 affected page loads) ~9KPeople who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~9,908 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 230,415 affected page loads)

Affected elements (2):

Element 1 DAP-b78616b0

Element path: ul > li > form > input#query-field

Snippet:

<input type="text" id="query-field" name="inputstring" size="12" value="Local Forecast by ZIP/City, State" onfocus="this.value='';" onblur="this.value='Local Forecast by ZIP/City, State';">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has insufficient color contrast of 2.84 (foreground color: #999999, background color: #ffffff, font size: 8.0pt (10.6667px), font weight: normal). Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1

<font color="#ff0000">

Label: Critical

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

`<frame>` or `<iframe>` elements do not have a title (rule: frame-title) DAP-43cf5d17

Screen reader users rely on frame titles to describe the contents of frames. Learn more about frame titles.

Disabilities affected: ~2KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~2,304 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 230,415 affected page loads) ~691People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-d0cdbcfd

Element path: tbody > tr > td#TopicContainer > iframe

Snippet:

<iframe src="//www.facebook.com/plugins/likebox.php?href=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.facebook.com…" scrolling="yes" frameborder="1" style="border:none; overflow:hidden; width:500px; height:395px;" allowtransparency="true">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has no title attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute (rule: html-has-lang) DAP-34fc29ea

If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. Learn more about the `lang` attribute.

Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt) DAP-84ac16d9

Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. Learn more about the `alt` attribute.

Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name) DAP-3f1e53d3

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size) DAP-6b652fcf

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Disabilities affected: ~5KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~5,069 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 230,415 affected page loads) ~13KPeople with limited reach, strength, or stamina. Estimated ~13,364 people potentially excluded (5.8% prevalence × 230,415 affected page loads)

Document does not have a main landmark. (rule: landmark-one-main) DAP-485fce88

One main landmark helps screen reader users navigate a web page. Learn more about landmarks.

Disabilities affected: ~2KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~2,304 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 230,415 affected page loads) ~691People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~691 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 230,415 affected page loads) ~5KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~5,069 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 230,415 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-ef8f516e

Element path: html

Snippet:

<html xmlns="//www.w3.org/1999/xhtml">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Document does not have a main landmark
`<td>` elements in a large `<table>` do not have table headers. (rule: td-has-header) DAP-42964e6b

Screen readers have features to make navigating tables easier. Ensuring that `<td>` elements in a large table (3 or more cells in width and height) have an associated table header may improve the experience for screen reader users. Learn more about table headers.

Disabilities affected: ~2KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~2,304 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 230,415 affected page loads) ~691People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~691 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 230,415 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-17306674

Element path: tbody > tr > td > table

Snippet:

<table cellspacing="1" cellpadding="2" border="0" width="100%">

Label: Hazard Fri (04/17) Sat (04/18) Sun (04/19) Mon (04/20) Tue (04/21) Wed (04/22) …

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Some non-empty data cells do not have table headers

Accessibility Findings

https://cdc.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 91

Axe findings: 3

Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name) DAP-3e7fec37

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~2KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~2,266 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 226,568 affected page loads) ~680People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~680 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 226,568 affected page loads) ~4KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~4,984 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 226,568 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-2428d6f9

Element path: nav.mobile-navbar > div.mobile-navbar_top > div.d-flex > a#cdc-mobile-search-toggle

Snippet:

<a id="cdc-mobile-search-toggle" class="nav-link" href="#" aria-controls="cdc-mobile-search-form">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Element is in tab order and does not have accessible text
  • Fix any of the following:
  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
Skip links are not focusable. (rule: skip-link) DAP-b3698d5b

Including a skip link can help users skip to the main content to save time. Learn more about skip links.

Disabilities affected: ~2KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~2,266 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 226,568 affected page loads) ~680People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~680 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 226,568 affected page loads) ~4KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~4,984 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 226,568 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-d7fc04fe

Element path: body.cdc-post-type--cdc-homepage > div#skipmenu > a.skippy

Snippet:

<a class="skippy visually-hidden-focusable" href="#headerSearch" tabindex="2">

Label: Skip to search

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • No skip link target
Some elements have a `[tabindex]` value greater than 0 (rule: tabindex) DAP-6eb16f7d

A value greater than 0 implies an explicit navigation ordering. Although technically valid, this often creates frustrating experiences for users who rely on assistive technologies. Learn more about the `tabindex` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~2KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~2,266 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 226,568 affected page loads) ~680People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~680 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 226,568 affected page loads) ~4KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~4,984 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 226,568 affected page loads)

Affected elements (3):

Element 1 DAP-2b0faadb

Element path: body.cdc-post-type--cdc-homepage > div#skipmenu > a.skippy

Snippet:

<a class="skippy visually-hidden-focusable" href="#content" tabindex="1">

Label: Skip to site content

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has a tabindex greater than 0

Element 2 DAP-2b0faadb

Element path: body.cdc-post-type--cdc-homepage > div#skipmenu > a.skippy

Snippet:

<a class="skippy visually-hidden-focusable" href="#headerSearch" tabindex="2">

Label: Skip to search

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has a tabindex greater than 0

Element 3 DAP-3994b993

Element path: div#hero-carousel > div.carousel-indicators > div.carousel-indicators-wrapper > button#animation-toggle

Snippet:

<button id="animation-toggle" aria-pressed="true" tabindex="3">

Label: Pause carousel

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has a tabindex greater than 0

Accessibility Findings

https://tsp.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 91

Axe findings: 2

Buttons do not have an accessible name (rule: button-name) DAP-94f6c92a

When a button doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it as "button", making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. Learn how to make buttons more accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~2KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~2,216 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 221,588 affected page loads) ~665People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~665 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 221,588 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-13779943

Element path: aside#tsp-toolbar > ul > li > button#toggle-tsp-zoom

Snippet:

<button id="toggle-tsp-zoom" class="tooltip">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have inner text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Links rely on color to be distinguishable. (rule: link-in-text-block) DAP-c0c32991

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Link text that is discernible improves the experience for users with low vision. Learn how to make links distinguishable.

Disabilities affected: ~5KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~5,318 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 221,588 affected page loads) ~9KPeople who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~9,528 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 221,588 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-dbbb92aa

Element path: div.usa-grid > div.usa-footer-logo > p > a

Snippet:

<a href="https://www.tsp.gov/contact/">

Label: contact us

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 1.5:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #d6d7d9, surrounding text: #aeb0b5)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text

Accessibility Findings

https://travel.state.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 93

Axe findings: 2

`[user-scalable="no"]` is used in the `<meta name="viewport">` element or the `[maximum-scale]` attribute is less than 5. (rule: meta-viewport) DAP-99efae35

Disabling zooming is problematic for users with low vision who rely on screen magnification to properly see the contents of a web page. Learn more about the viewport meta tag.

Disabilities affected: ~4KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~4,726 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 196,913 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-06ade13b

Element path: head > meta

Snippet:

<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, maximum-scale=1.0, user-scalable=no">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • user-scalable=no on <meta> tag disables zooming on mobile devices
Document does not have a main landmark. (rule: landmark-one-main) DAP-b55de0fc

One main landmark helps screen reader users navigate a web page. Learn more about landmarks.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1,969 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 196,913 affected page loads) ~591People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~4,332 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 196,913 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-59c242b4

Element path: html

Snippet:

<html lang="en">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Document does not have a main landmark

Accessibility Findings

https://ceac.state.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 94

Axe findings: 2

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order) DAP-58f8b5b4

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Accessibility Findings

https://medlineplus.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 95

Axe findings: 2

Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size) DAP-80cca99a

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Disabilities affected: ~3KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~3,022 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 137,373 affected page loads) ~7KPeople with limited reach, strength, or stamina. Estimated ~7,968 people potentially excluded (5.8% prevalence × 137,373 affected page loads)

Affected elements (16):

Element 1 DAP-e1d41724

Element path: div.footer1 > ul.secondarynav > li > a

Snippet:

<a href="https://medlineplus.gov/about/">

Label: About MedlinePlus

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (110.9px by 21.4px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 21.4px instead of at least 24px.

Accessibility Findings

https://esta.cbp.dhs.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 85

Axe findings: 4

Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt) DAP-72867807

Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. Learn more about the `alt` attribute.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~1,159 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 115,920 affected page loads) ~348People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~348 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 115,920 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-27009710

Element path: div.toggle-language > span.dropdown > button#languageDrop > img

Snippet:

<img _ngcontent-ng-c2420648736="" src="assets/images/flags/usUkFlag.png">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have an alt attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Links rely on color to be distinguishable. (rule: link-in-text-block) DAP-4d2fde10

Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. Link text that is discernible improves the experience for users with low vision. Learn how to make links distinguishable.

Disabilities affected: ~2KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~2,782 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 115,920 affected page loads) ~4KPeople who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Estimated ~4,985 people potentially excluded (4.3% prevalence × 115,920 affected page loads)

Element 1 DAP-a5b23db0

Element path: div.col-xs-12 > ul > li#welcome.whoYouAre.1 > a.strong

Snippet:

<a tabindex="0" data-target="#visaModal" data-toggle="modal" class="strong esta-link" href="#">

Label: Visa Waiver Program

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The link has insufficient color contrast of 1.18:1 with the surrounding text. (Minimum contrast is 3:1, link text: #00416a, surrounding text: #333333)
  • The link has no styling (such as underline) to distinguish it from the surrounding text

  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty

  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>

  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

Element 1 DAP-163e8874

Element path: div#sbar > form#f > div > input

Snippet:

<input type="submit" value="Search">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has insufficient color contrast of 4.11 (foreground color: #ffffff, background color: #357ae8, font size: 10.0pt (13.3333px), font weight: bold). Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1
`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute (rule: html-has-lang) DAP-415613a4

If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. Learn more about the `lang` attribute.

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The <html> element does not have a lang attribute
Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size) DAP-ef8eddce

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (16px by 16px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 18px instead of at least 24px.
Document does not have a main landmark. (rule: landmark-one-main) DAP-c41d9709

One main landmark helps screen reader users navigate a web page. Learn more about landmarks.

Accessibility Findings

https://radar.weather.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 77

Axe findings: 2

`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute (rule: html-has-lang) DAP-63fbd253

If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. Learn more about the `lang` attribute.

Estimated ~3,514 people potentially excluded (4.7% prevalence × 74,773 affected page loads)

Element 1 DAP-be88516d

Element path: html

Snippet:

<html>

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • The <html> element does not have a lang attribute
`[user-scalable="no"]` is used in the `<meta name="viewport">` element or the `[maximum-scale]` attribute is less than 5. (rule: meta-viewport) DAP-94dc23bb

Disabling zooming is problematic for users with low vision who rely on screen magnification to properly see the contents of a web page. Learn more about the viewport meta tag.

Disabilities affected: ~1KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~1,795 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 74,773 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-36f6c272

Element path: head > meta

Snippet:

<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1, maximum-scale=1, user-scalable=0">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • user-scalable on <meta> tag disables zooming on mobile devices

Some elements have a `[tabindex]` value greater than 0 (rule: tabindex) DAP-46a39875

A value greater than 0 implies an explicit navigation ordering. Although technically valid, this often creates frustrating experiences for users who rely on assistive technologies. Learn more about the `tabindex` attribute.

Element 4 DAP-9052a7fc

Element path: div.field__item > div.paragraph > div.ds-u-lg-display--none > button#trigger-tabs-902554153

Snippet:

<button type="button" id="trigger-tabs-902554153" aria-expanded="false" class="responsive-tabs__tab-button--mobile ds-u-display--flex" role="tab" aria-controls="tabs-902554153">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Required ARIA parent role not present: tablist

Element 5 DAP-b90c6a80

Element path: div.field__item > div.paragraph > div.ds-u-lg-display--none > button#trigger-tabs-223435921

Snippet:

<button type="button" id="trigger-tabs-223435921" aria-expanded="false" class="responsive-tabs__tab-button--mobile ds-u-display--flex" role="tab" aria-controls="tabs-223435921">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Required ARIA parent role not present: tablist
Buttons do not have an accessible name (rule: button-name) DAP-756f8154

When a button doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it as "button", making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. Learn how to make buttons more accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~559People who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~559 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 55,857 affected page loads) ~168People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~168 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 55,857 affected page loads)

Affected elements (5):

Element 1 DAP-10088305

Element path: div.field__item > div.paragraph > div.ds-u-lg-display--none > button#trigger-tabs-183003323

Snippet:

<button type="button" id="trigger-tabs-183003323" aria-expanded="false" class="responsive-tabs__tab-button--mobile ds-u-display--flex" role="tab" aria-controls="tabs-183003323">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have inner text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

Download full axe findings JSON | Download full axe findings CSV

Accessibility Findings

https://exclusions.oig.hhs.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 70

Axe findings: 6

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order) DAP-2ea6211b

Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. Learn more about heading order.

Disabilities affected: ~554People who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~554 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 55,378 affected page loads) ~166People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~166 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 55,378 affected page loads) ~1KPeople with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~1,218 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 55,378 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-fec22b23

Element path: div > div#content > div.related > h3

Snippet:

<h3>

Label: Related Content

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Heading order invalid
Input buttons do not have discernible text. (rule: input-button-name) DAP-5e8a9d33

Adding discernable and accessible text to input buttons may help screen reader users understand the purpose of the input button. Learn more about input buttons.

Disabilities affected: ~554People who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~554 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 55,378 affected page loads) ~166People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~166 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 55,378 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-b25ed5e3

Element path: tbody > tr > td > input#btnG

Snippet:

<input id="btnG" name="searchButton" class="topSearchButton" type="submit" value="" target="search_iframe">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has a value attribute and the value attribute is empty
  • Element has an empty value attribute
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
  • Element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  • Element does not have an explicit <label>
  • Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
Form elements do not have associated labels (rule: label) DAP-0a52ceea

Labels ensure that form controls are announced properly by assistive technologies, like screen readers. Learn more about form element labels.

Disabilities affected: ~554People who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~554 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 55,378 affected page loads) ~1KPeople with low vision who need magnification or high contrast.   • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty

Some elements have a `[tabindex]` value greater than 0 (rule: tabindex) DAP-cf49e797

A value greater than 0 implies an explicit navigation ordering. Although technically valid, this often creates frustrating experiences for users who rely on assistive technologies. Learn more about the `tabindex` attribute.

Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size) DAP-bfe9fc71

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Estimated ~1,218 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 55,378 affected page loads) ~3KPeople with limited reach, strength, or stamina. Affected elements (30):

Element 1 DAP-ec254e23

Element path: div#textReset > div#header > div#changeFont > a.increaseFont

Snippet:

<a class="increaseFont" href="#">

Label: +

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (10.5px by 20px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 6.2px instead of at least 24px.

Some ARIA parent roles must contain specific child roles to perform their intended accessibility functions. Learn more about roles and required children elements.

Accessibility Findings

https://donotcall.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 80

Axe findings: 3

`<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute (rule: html-has-lang) DAP-c52e7ff5

If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. Learn more about the `lang` attribute.

Some elements have a `[tabindex]` value greater than 0 (rule: tabindex) DAP-d254a2a4

A value greater than 0 implies an explicit navigation ordering. Although technically valid, this often creates frustrating experiences for users who rely on assistive technologies. Learn more about the `tabindex` attribute.

Links do not have a discernible name (rule: link-name) DAP-9cb9ebbe

Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. Learn how to make links accessible.

Disabilities affected: ~409People who are blind or have no functional vision. Estimated ~409 people potentially excluded (1.0% prevalence × 40,882 affected page loads) ~123People who are deaf and cannot hear audio content. Estimated ~123 people potentially excluded (0.3% prevalence × 40,882 affected page loads) ~899People with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~899 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 40,882 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-8042e6fb

Element path: div.row > div.col-12 > div.recall-alert--links > a.link-arrow

Snippet:

<a href="/vehicle-safety/takata-recall-spotlight" class="link-arrow">

How to fix: Fix all of the following:

  • Element is in tab order and does not have accessible text
  • Fix any of the following:
  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute
Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size) DAP-045fb615

Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. Learn more about touch targets.

Disabilities affected: ~899People with limited hand, finger, or fine motor dexterity. Estimated ~899 people potentially excluded (2.2% prevalence × 40,882 affected page loads) ~2KPeople with limited reach, strength, or stamina. Estimated ~2,371 people potentially excluded (5.8% prevalence × 40,882 affected page loads)

Affected elements (1):

Element 1 DAP-b0a8b77c

Element path: div.col-lg-3 > div.footer-site--tel-links-container > span > a

Snippet:

<a href="tel:888-327-4236">

Label: 888-327-4236

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Target has insufficient size (98.8px by 19px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  • Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 23.6px instead of at least 24px.
Elements with visible text labels do not have matching accessible names. (rule: label-content-name-mismatch) DAP-a903365f

Visible text labels that do not match the accessible name can result in a confusing experience for screen reader users. Learn more about accessible names.

Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes (rule: image-alt) DAP-e60c9bef

Disabilities affected: ~858People with low vision who need magnification or high contrast. Estimated ~858 people potentially excluded (2.4% prevalence × 35,746 affected page loads) ~1KPeople who cannot distinguish certain colors (color blindness). Affected elements (2):

Element 1 DAP-391cafef

Element path: form > div.row > div.home__form > input.btn

Snippet:

<input type="submit" value="Submit" class="btn btn-gold my-0">

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has insufficient color contrast of 3.33 (foreground color: #ffffff, background color: #b0862c, font size: 12.0pt (16px), font weight: normal). Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1

How to fix: Fix any of the following:

  • Element has insufficient color contrast of 3.33 (foreground color: #ffffff, background color: #b0862c, font size: 12.0pt (16px), font weight: normal). Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1
Some elements have a `[tabindex]` value greater than 0 (rule: tabindex) DAP-b6e82c3e

A value greater than 0 implies an explicit navigation ordering. Although technically valid, this often creates frustrating experiences for users who rely on assistive technologies. Learn more about the `tabindex` attribute.

  • List element has direct children that are not allowed: div
List items (`<li>`) are not contained within `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` parent elements. (rule: listitem) DAP-4ead3bcb

Screen readers require list items (`<li>`) to be contained within a parent `<ul>`, `<ol>` or `<menu>` to be announced properly. Learn more about proper list structure.

Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing. (rule: target-size) DAP-8b8fb577

Accessibility Findings

https://usitc.gov

Lighthouse Accessibility Score: 95

Axe findings: 3

`[aria-hidden="true"]` elements contain focusable descendents (rule: aria-hidden-focus) DAP-1ada2d27

Focusable descendents within an `[aria-hidden="true"]` element prevent those interactive elements from being available to users of assistive technologies like screen readers. Learn how `aria-hidden` affects focusable elements.

Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order (rule: heading-order) DAP-a04d28fe

`[aria-*]` attributes do not match their roles (rule: aria-allowed-attr) DAP-3fb09f9a

Each ARIA `role` supports a specific subset of `aria-*` attributes. Mismatching these invalidates the `aria-*` attributes. Learn how to match ARIA attributes to their roles.
